signals.reasoning: Cover the live BTC short. It is still green, but it has already slipped to about 53% peak PnL retention, is carrying an active giveback warning, and still has no stop while price is stabilizing rather than extending lower. This is protect-the-remainder cleanup, not a hold.
ETH remains weak on the higher timeframe, but the current action is more rebound than breakdown and the macro veto is still active. Not cleaner than simply cleaning up the live BTC short.
SOL is bouncing modestly after the flush and no longer offers a clean immediate short trigger. Under veto, this is a watchlist name, not a fresh action point.
BLAST still leans bearish on the board, but the tape is too thin and already flushed enough that forcing a fresh short is lower quality than it looks.

LIT has re-accelerated upward intraday and no longer behaves like a clean short setup. Even though the mechanical board still flags fresh shorts pressing, the live tape has become too unstable to trust right now.
IP is still a weak bounce inside a damaged structure, but the edge is not strong enough to justify action under the current event brake.
FTT is too thin and too structurally noisy to recommend. It is exactly the kind of misleading board name that should stay watch-only.
MERL remains soft, but the move still reads as an already-developed flush rather than a clean active continuation entry. No fresh trade.
VINE scores as bearish on paper, but the liquidity is weak and the macro veto is active. That makes it a watchlist short, not a trustworthy active recommendation.
GOAT has already suffered a severe washout and now carries too much squeeze risk to be a responsible fresh short. Not worth forcing.
0G is still the strongest squeeze name on the sheet, but it is already extended, crowded, and highly event-sensitive. Under veto, that is not enough to promote into a real trade.
